category_title: Struggling readers who use collaborative talk to clarify character motives and adapt schema
scenario_title: Reconciling complex character motives during literature discussion
minimal_context: The student is participating in a literature discussion group about the novel 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' to analyze character motives.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at your booklet and discuss the chapter in 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' where the mailman delivers the box without an adult's signature.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Did the mom really want Jeremy to have the box?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think about her motives?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Yeah, but Oswald said that, um, that mail dude was following along with it because he…

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep reading the text to see if you can figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Skims the pictures in the text) I'm confused by it. I get to the point that I barely understand it. I have a lot of stuff in my head that I don't know what it means.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at your booklet and discuss the chapter in 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' where the mailman delivers the box without an adult's signature.

STUDENT: Did the mom really want Jeremy to have the box?

TUTOR: What do you think about her motives?

STUDENT: Yeah, but Oswald said that, um, that mail dude was following along with it because he…

TUTOR: Keep reading the text to see if you can figure it out.

STUDENT: (Skims the pictures in the text) I'm confused by it. I get to the point that I barely understand it. I have a lot of stuff in my head that I don't know what it means.

task.content: Participating in a literature discussion group (LDG) focused on analyzing character motives in the novel 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life', specifically discussing why the mailman delivered a box without an adult's signature and Jeremy's mom's motives.
task.learning_goal: Synthesizing information across chapters of a novel during a peer discussion to understand complex character actions and authorial intent.

context.materials: Novel 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life', handmade booklets made of four 8.5 x 11 in. sheets of copy paper, folded in half and stapled down the middle with construction paper covers
context.social_setting: Literature discussion group with heterogeneous peers including Donnie, a student who dislikes school reading, and Ray, an honors student

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When her schema had to readjust and adapt to new information from this chapter regarding adult characters' motives
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Questioned Jeremy’s mother’s motives to be sure she understood what was happening in the chapter
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Having a lot of stuff in my head that I don't know what it means
related_quotes: Did the mom really want Jeremy to have the box?
Yeah, but Oswald said that, um, that mail dude was following along with it because he…
Oh, and he [the mailman] could be sneaky about him [Jeremy] trying to find out what it [the box] was.
b/c [because] it cleans my head out because if we don’t do it [literature discussion] then I have a lot of stuff in my head that I don't know what it means.

behavior_patterns.condition: When encountering complex thinking or hard words in content area text
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Disengaged from reading, choosing to either skim the text pictures and captions or spend large amounts of time 'finding' a book
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Confused by it, don't get it, barely understand it
related_quotes: get to the point that I barely understand it
some of the words are hard.
